title: Zoro Faces the World’s Strongest Swordsman
description: On pages 5–8, Zoro confronts Dracule Mihawk, forcing his dream of becoming the world’s greatest swordsman into a brutal and public test.
scene: The Baratie battle pauses around a duel that dwarfs the immediate conflict: Zoro faces the gap between ambition and the world’s strongest swordsman.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: His signature attack that sends his opponents flying 100% was stopped!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I... can't move... there hasn't been a single man who could see through that move until now! So how can he stop me with that toy...!?!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's not possible for the world to be this far away!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: There shouldn't be such a vast difference between us! There can't be!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.text: Come on, bro! show him what you've really got!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: He's easily taking on Roronoa Zoro's 3 swords with that little thing!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I didn't train so hard until today,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Just to fight against this stupid little toy...!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: You're lucky. You're a boy, Zoro.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...I wanted to become the world's best too!

summary: This part is a defining moment for Zoro, showing that his dream is real enough to risk death and that his loyalty to Luffy survives defeat.
